item.description:
1-3 Creating a survey and fetching it, both as a list and by its id.

A new survey is created in DRAFT status.

4 Editing a survey's name and description.

5-6 Activating and deactivating the survey, each confirmed with a message.

A survey cannot be activated unless it has at least one active question.

7 Deleting the survey, the record is kept but hidden.

8 Fetching a survey that does not exist returns a 404.

item.name: 2. Questions Management

body.raw:

item.description:
1 Creating a fresh survey to hold the questions.  
2-3 Adding a text question and a choice question with its options.  
4 Fetching the survey's questions; an admin flag also reveals inactive ones.  
5-6 Editing a question, including rebuilding a choice question's option list.  
Options are added, relabeled, and removed through the question edit itself.  
7-8 Deactivating and reactivating a question a deactivated question stays visible to the admin.  
9 Deleting a question

item.name: 3. Response Submission
3A. Intern Response. Festus Kiprono

exec: try { const j = xml2Json(pm.response.text()); if (j && j.survey && j.survey.id) sid = j.survey.id; } catch (e) {}
if (!sid) { const m = pm.response.text().match(/<id>(\d+)<\/id>/); if (m) sid = m[1]; }
if (sid) { pm.collectionVariables.set('internSurveyId', String(sid)); console.log('Intern survey id =', sid); }
pm.test('Intern: survey created & id captured', function () { pm.expect(sid, 'no <id> in response').to.not.be.undefined; });

item.name: 3A.9 Submit response (Festus Kiprono + CV + Cover Letter)

exec: for (const p of patterns) { const m = t.match(p); if (m) { cid = m[1]; break; } }

item.description:
The full intern applicant flow.

3A.1 Creates the Backend Intern survey and stores its id in {{internSurveyId}}.

3A.2-3A.7 Add the six questions.

3A.8 Activates it.

3A.9 Submits Festus Kiprono's answers with two PDF uploads.

Before sending 3A.9, click the cv and cover_letter file rows and pick a PDF for each.

The certificate id from the upload is captured into {{certificateId}} for Folder 4's download step.

item.name: 3B. Experienced Response. Brian Keneth

exec: try { const j = xml2Json(pm.response.text()); if (j && j.survey && j.survey.id) sid = j.survey.id; } catch (e) {}
if (!sid) { const m = pm.response.text().match(/<id>(\d+)<\/id>/); if (m) sid = m[1]; }
if (sid) { pm.collectionVariables.set('expSurveyId', String(sid)); console.log('Experienced survey id =', sid); }
pm.test('Experienced: survey created & id captured', function () { pm.expect(sid, 'no <id> in response').to.not.be.undefined; });

body.raw:
<survey>
    <name>Backend Developer (Mid Level)</name>
    <description>Screening survey for the mid-level backend developer position</description>
</survey>

<question>
    <name>full_name</name>
    <prompt>What is your full name?</prompt>
    <description>[Surname] [First Name] [Other Names]</description>
    <type>short_text</type>
    <required>true</required>
    <maxLength>150</maxLength>
</question>

<question>
    <name>email_address</name>
    <prompt>What is your email address?</prompt>
    <description></description>
    <type>email</type>
    <required>true</required>
</question>

<question>
    <name>programming_stack</name>
    <prompt>What programming stack are you familiar with?</prompt>
    <description>You can select multiple</description>
    <type>choice</type>
    <required>true</required>
    <allowMultiple>true</allowMultiple>
    <minSelection>1</minSelection>
    <maxSelection>4</maxSelection>
    <options>
        <options>
            <value>JAVA_SPRING</value>
            <label>Java Spring</label>
        </options>
        <options>
            <value>PYTHON_FASTAPI</value>
            <label>Python FastAPI</label>
        </options>
        <options>
            <value>NODE</value>
            <label>Node.js</label>
        </options>
        <options>
            <value>GO</value>
            <label>Go</label>
        </options>
    </options>
</question>

<question>
    <name>years_experience</name>
    <prompt>How many years of professional backend experience do you have?</prompt>
    <description>Select one</description>
    <type>choice</type>
    <required>true</required>
    <allowMultiple>false</allowMultiple>
    <minSelection>1</minSelection>
    <maxSelection>1</maxSelection>
    <options>
        <options>
            <value>ONE_TWO</value>
            <label>1-2 years</label>
        </options>
        <options>
            <value>THREE_FIVE</value>
            <label>3-5 years</label>
        </options>
        <options>
            <value>SIX_PLUS</value>
            <label>6+ years</label>
        </options>
    </options>
</question>

<question>
    <name>github_url</name>
    <prompt>Share a link to your GitHub or portfolio</prompt>
    <description>Full URL</description>
    <type>short_text</type>
    <required>false</required>
    <maxLength>250</maxLength>
</question>

<question>
    <name>resume</name>
    <prompt>Upload your resume</prompt>
    <description>PDF only (.pdf), max 2MB</description>
    <type>file</type>
    <required>true</required>
    <fileFormat>pdf</fileFormat>
    <maxFileSize>2</maxFileSize>
    <maxFileSizeUnit>mb</maxFileSizeUnit>
    <allowMultipleFiles>false</allowMultipleFiles>
</question>

item.description:
The full experienced applicant flow.

3B.1 Creates the Backend Developer (Mid Level) survey and stores its id in {{expSurveyId}}s

3B.2-3B.7 Add six questions ( single-select years_experience and optional github_url).

3B.8 Activates the survey

3B.9 Submits Brian Keneth's answers. Before sending 3B.9, pick a PDF on the resume file row.

Two complete applicant journeys so the whole process is visible end to end.

3A - Intern: Festus Kiprono, submits CV + Cover Letter.
3B - Experienced: Brian Keneth, mid-level developer.

Each sub-folder builds its own survey, adds its questions, activates, then submits a response. Survey ids are captured automatically, so run each sub-folder top to bottom - no ids to paste by hand. A survey cannot be activated until it has at least one active question, which is why activation comes after the questions.

item.name: 4. Response Management
5 Filter by email (no match, empty page)

item.description: Admin review of what Folder 3A submitted. Requests 1-5 read Festus's response from {{internSurveyId}}, including pagination and an email filter that matches (festus@gmail.com) and one that does not. Request 6 downloads the certificate uploaded in 3A using the captured {{certificateId}}. Requests 7-8 show the 404s for a missing certificate and a missing survey. Run Folder 3A first so the variables are set.
